variant: give me a morning skincare checklist i can actually follow

request.message: give me a morning skincare checklist i can actually follow

response_body.response: here's your AM checklist. keep it simple—most skin problems come from doing too much, not too little.  the order matters. cleanser first so actives actually reach skin, then hydrate on damp skin to expand cells and let the next layers absorb better. skip the active on days you're not using it,don't stack acids or retinoids. SPF is the one thing you can't skip; UV worsens pigmentation and breaks down collagen. what's your skin type (oily, dry, combo, sensitive)? that'll let me dial in which active and moisturizer actually fit you.

products.name: The Ordinary Azelaic Acid Suspension 10%
products.description: Azelaic acid for redness, PIH, and mild rosacea — pregnancy-safe.

products.name: CeraVe PM Facial Moisturizing Lotion
products.description: Lightweight ceramide + niacinamide moisturizer for nightly use.

items: cleanse with water or gentle cleanser
apply toner or hydrating mist on damp skin (optional but helps absorption)
one active: azelaic acid 3–5x/week, or skip on off days
treatment serum: vitamin c or hydrating serum
